forecast.name: Overnight
forecast.detailedForecast: A slight chance of drizzle. Mostly cloudy. Low around 57, with temperatures rising to around 59 overnight. Southwest wind around 9 mph.

forecast.name: Juneteenth
forecast.shortForecast: Slight Chance Drizzle then Mostly Sunny
forecast.detailedForecast: A slight chance of drizzle before 11am. Mostly sunny. High near 65, with temperatures falling to around 63 in the afternoon. Southwest wind 7 to 15 mph, with gusts as high as 22 mph.

weather_report:
As you step into the early hours of Juneteenth in Downtown San Francisco, a gentle veil of clouds lingers overhead, a soft blanket that promises a slow transition into the morning. The air carries the faint hint of drizzle and an invitation to embrace the coolness of the night. With temperatures hovering around the mid-50s, a light jacket or sweater would be a sensible companion, providing a layer of comfort against the brisk southwest wind that brushes past at roughly nine miles per hour.

As the day unfolds, anticipation builds for a shift in the atmosphere. By late morning, that slight chance of drizzle is expected to dissipate, making way for the sun to peek through the clouds and cast a warm glow across the city. The high is projected to reach into the mid-60s, though expect a gentle decline in temperature as afternoon approaches, nudging the thermometer down a notch. With gusts reaching up to 22 mph, it would be wise to secure any loose hats or papers you might be carrying. 

Despite the promise of sunshine, the marine layer will remain a specter, clinging to the hills and occasionally spilling into the streets with whispers of fog. Yet the day holds beauty—ideal for meandering through the cafes or perhaps finding solace in a quiet park, watching the city stir to life, its rhythm redefined with each passing moment. As time drifts gradually toward midday, consider indulging in a leisurely stroll to savor an artisanal coffee or a bite from one of the local food trucks. Today calls for exploration tempered with the serenity that only a San Francisco morning can provide.
